narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings—pouring your heart into law school dreams only to have them paused like this feels deeply unfair, and I'm here to walk you through why our prediction model saw your path to the bar exam the way it did, like sharing the chapters of a story that shaped its view of yours. At the top of what stood out was your Year 3 decile of 1.0, the strongest signal pulling toward a tougher road ahead, as it placed you at the lower end of your school's performance there, hinting at challenges in building momentum. Right behind that was your LSAT score of 27.0, which the model weighed heavily as another key factor tipping toward failure, suggesting it might not fully equip you for the bar's intense demands. Your Year 1 decile of 1.0 reinforced this pattern, painting a picture of early struggles that carried forward. Even with your high family income, which often opens doors, it surprisingly nudged the outlook downward in this mix, perhaps because the model saw it alongside other hurdles rather than as a clear buffer. Your Asian background and undergraduate GPA of 2.9 added layers, each contributing their pull toward that 45% failure risk, while being male and full-time rounded out a profile where these elements combined into a riskier narrative. It's not that any one piece defines you—your full-time commitment shows real drive—but together, they formed a story the model couldn't ignore, one where the bar passage odds felt too uncertain for admission right now. This isn't the end of your story; it's a nudge to strengthen those early chapters, maybe through targeted prep or boosting metrics like LSAT or grades. You've got high family support as a foundation—lean into it to rewrite what comes next. We're rooting for your comeback.
